title: Mandatory Publishing, Returns, Alternate Exchanges, and Route Audits · Compiler Case: Route Audit Fixture
summary: The service publishes with mandatory false, treats confirms as proof of routing, attaches the return listener after publishing, and retries unroutable messages forever without fixing topology. Planned artifact: Add and verify the route audit fixture increment in unroutable publication detection and ownership record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build topology fixtures that assert expected destination sets, forbidden routes, unroutable cases, permissions, and changed bindings.
Distinguish broker confirms from consumer processing and combine mandatory returns or alternate routing with confirm results.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.instruction:
